title: The International Consensus Classification of Mature Lymphoid Neoplasms: a report from the Clinical Advisory Committee
design: Consensus classification / diagnostic-criteria report from an international Clinical Advisory Committee (14 working groups), updating lymphoid neoplasm classification with genomic data.
population: N/A (diagnostic classification of mature lymphoid, histiocytic, and dendritic cell neoplasms).
key_eligibility: Defines diagnostic entities and required ancillary studies (e.g., FISH) for classification
High-grade B-cell lymphoma with MYC and BCL2 rearrangements category redefined to EXCLUDE cases with only MYC and BCL6 rearrangements (those become a separate provisional entity)
outcomes: Establishes 'High-grade B-cell lymphoma with MYC and BCL2 rearrangements' (HGBCL-DH-BCL2, 'double-hit') as a defined entity of GCB origin, diagnosed by demonstrating both MYC and BCL2 rearrangements (translocations detected by FISH). Critically, copy-number increase or amplification of MYC/BCL2 is explicitly NOT sufficient to substitute for a true rearrangement. Double-hit categories continue to exclude follicular lymphoma; the MYC partner gene (IG vs non-IG) does not affect classification. HGBCL-DH-BCL6 (MYC+BCL6) is a separate provisional entity.
grounds_rubric_claims: Double-hit lymphoma = concurrent MYC AND BCL2 rearrangements (translocations), demonstrated by FISH
Copy-number gain or amplification of MYC/BCL2 does NOT qualify as a rearrangement and cannot substitute for it in the double-hit definition
MYC+BCL6 (without BCL2) is a separate provisional entity, not the classic MYC/BCL2 double-hit

verbatim_quotes.quote: HGBCL-DH now comprises 2 groups: HGBCL with MYC and BCL2 rearrangements (with or without BCL6 rearrangement) (HGBCL-DH-BCL2) and a new provisional entity, HBGBL with MYC and BCL6 rearrangements (HGBCL-DH-BCL6).
verbatim_quotes.supports: Double-hit defined by MYC and BCL2 rearrangement (distinct from MYC/BCL6)

verbatim_quotes.quote: Neither copy number increase nor amplification of these genes is sufficient to substitute for rearrangement in these categories.
verbatim_quotes.supports: Amplification/copy-number gain does NOT count as double-hit; only true rearrangement qualifies

verbatim_quotes.quote: The category is redefined to exclude patients with only MYC and BCL6 rearrangements.
verbatim_quotes.supports: MYC/BCL6-only cases are excluded from the classic MYC+BCL2 double-hit category

caveats: ICC classification runs parallel to (and diverges in places from) the concurrent WHO-HAEM5; terminology and provisional-entity status (e.g., HGBCL-DH-BCL6) may differ between systems. Requires FISH; MYC partner-gene significance remains controversial.
